title: Exact q-weighted geometric series
statement: For a good block coordinate with B = qM + k, 0 <= k < M, and B > M, we have 1/M = q/(B-k) = Σ_{j>=0} qk^j / B^(j+1).
proof_status: Lean theorems in QRTour.OrbitWeave formalize the good-mode fraction identity, the finite partial-sum identity, and the geometric-series expansion.

title: Order of a power
statement: If r = ord_n(a), then ord_n(a^m) = r / gcd(r, m).
proof_status: used explicitly in Python and indirectly in Lean classification

title: Exact QR-generator stride classification
statement: For an odd prime p and h = (p-1)/2, the stride m is QR-generating for base B exactly when ord_p(B) / gcd(ord_p(B), m) = h. Therefore QR-generating strides exist iff ord_p(B) is h or 2h, and their count is φ(h).
repo_status: fully formalized in Lean at the base level and backed by Python counterexample/regression tests
proof_status: formalized in Lean as pow_isQRGenerator_iff, pow_isQRGenerator_iff_coprime_half, qrGenerator_pow_count_eq_totient, and the base-level full-order reduction theorems culminating in base_qrGenerator_pow_count_eq_totient

title: Digit periodicity from the remainder orbit
statement: For an odd prime p and a base B coprime to p, the base-B digits of 1/p are periodic with period ord_p(B). At each step, the digit is the Euclidean quotient in B*r_n = d_n*p + r_{n+1}.
repo_status: formalized in Lean as the digit/remainder equation together with the exact digit periodicity theorem, and linked from the Agda correspondence audit
proof_status: Lean theorems in QRTour.Digits formalize the Euclidean digit/remainder equation, the digit bound, the period defined by orderOf on units, and the exact digit periodicity theorem.

title: Signed bridge recurrence and two-step cancellation
statement: If a prime denominator satisfies B^k ≡ ±d (mod p), then the remainder orbit obeys r[n+k] = (±d)r[n]. Consequently r[n+2k] = d^2 r[n], so the sign always cancels after two stride steps.
repo_status: formalized in Lean as the unified plus/minus bridge recurrence layer and promoted as the public signed-bridge theorem surface
proof_status: Lean theorems in QRTour.SignedBridge formalize the signed k-step recurrence, the two-step sign-cancellation law, and the conversion of ordinary bridge coordinates into the minus branch of the signed package.

title: Bridge block values are geometric and periodic
statement: For a bridge coordinate with B^k ≡ d (mod p), the block values at positions j*k are exactly d^j in ZMod p. Therefore the block sequence is periodic with period ord_p(d).
repo_status: formalized in Lean as the bridge block-value geometric sequence together with its exact order-theoretic periodicity
proof_status: Lean theorems in QRTour.PAdicBridge formalize the block-value geometric identity, define bridgeOrder as the order of d in the unit group, and prove that the block sequence repeats with period bridgeOrder.

title: Composite period via CRT
statement: If M = Π p_i^{e_i} is coprime to the base B, then ord_M(B) is the least common multiple of the local orders ord_{p_i^{e_i}}(B).
repo_status: finite prime-power CRT order theorem formalized in Lean and implemented computationally in Python
proof_status: formalized in Lean via the finite-product order theorem orderOf_pi and the prime-power CRT theorem orderOf_unitsEquivPrimePowers; the pairwise theorem orderOf_unitsChineseRemainder remains as the two-factor core

title: Preperiod from stripped base factors
statement: For denominator N and base b = Π p_i^{f_i}, the preperiod length is max_i ceil(v_{p_i}(N)/f_i); stripping those base factors leaves the purely periodic modulus M.
repo_status: implemented, tested, and globally formalized in Lean at the base-prime support level, with the stripping factor now exposed to the composite-visibility family layer
proof_status: Lean formalizes the local ceiling-valuation theorem, the global max-over-base-primes step count, the positivity and self-divisibility of the base-supported factor, and the divisibility of that factor into base^preperiodSteps; the stripped-modulus M statement itself remains classical/computational

title: Carry as a deterministic windowed transducer
statement: For a finite coefficient word c_0, ..., c_{L-1}, carry propagation can be modeled by a deterministic transducer whose state is the incoming carry from less significant blocks.
repo_status: explicit Python model with state graphs, coarse minimization hooks, carry-vs-remainder comparison reports, tests, and a Lean finite-window normalization/comparison surface
proof_status: construction-level result with explicit implemented/open boundary reporting; Lean now formalizes the finite normalization recursion, traced finite runs with per-step Euclidean semantics, exact fixed-window carry/remainder trace alignment, and certified finite output agreement, but not the global DFA factorization layer

title: Small residue improves visual legibility
statement: Small k, and especially q = 1, tends to postpone visible carry interactions and make the early block skeleton easier to read.

title: Good coordinates require positive q
statement: A mode m only gives a usable q-weighted block coordinate when B = base^m exceeds the periodic modulus M, so q = (B-k)/M is positive.
repo_status: formalized in Lean and implemented in search guards
proof_status: Lean theorem BlockCoordinate.positive_q_good_modes proves q = (B-k)/M is positive on good modes

title: Exact incoming-carry boundary from the geometric tail
statement: For a block coordinate B = qN + k with 0 < k < B, the incoming carry into block j of the raw coefficient stream qk^j equals floor(qk^(j+1)/(B-k)). Therefore the first incoming-carry position is the least j with qk^(j+1) >= B-k.
repo_status: formalized in Lean and mirrored in the Python visibility layer
proof_status: Lean theorems in QRTour.Visibility formalize the exact carry quotient, the positivity threshold, the least-position boundary predicate, and the fixed-window gap certificate that sits above the same carry-tail arithmetic.

title: Same-core threshold shifts are controlled by q-ratio intervals
statement: Fix a block coordinate with shared B and k, and compare an actual denominator to its stripped periodic core. If k^s <= (q_core / q_actual) < k^(s+1), then the incoming-carry and local-overflow thresholds for the core occur earlier by either s or s+1 blocks. When q_core / q_actual = k^s exactly, the shift is exactly s blocks. In the non-power interval case, Lean also packages scaled-raw-coefficient sufficient criteria for the lower and upper endpoint labels at fixed boundary data.
repo_status: formalized in Lean and implemented/tested in the visibility family layer
proof_status: Lean theorems in QRTour.Visibility prove the generic adjacent-threshold interval law and the exact k-power shift law, while QRTour.CompositeVisibility now packages those results for actual-versus-stripped-core families, characterizes same-core compatibility by exact quotient scaling, gives a practical near-denominator q = 1 coordinate-selection criterion, and adds exact/lower/upper endpoint labels together with scaled-raw-coefficient sufficient criteria for the non-power same-core cases.

title: Exact visibility threshold for carried prefixes
statement: There is a sharp arithmetic criterion, in terms of q, k, B, and the period data, for the minimal stabilization lookahead and the remaining global visibility behavior beyond the now-implemented incoming-carry and raw-prefix boundary formulas.
repo_status: tracked with exact observables, Lean formalization of the fixed-window gap certificate, its necessary tail-mass lower bound, monotone transport to larger lookahead windows, certified visible-word agreement, exact same-core visible-mismatch transport in the k-power regime, exact same-core transport of the raw tail-mass inequality and shifted coarse sufficient criterion, canonical case families, and exported visibility datasets; the least/global lookahead formula remains open
proof_status: Lean now formalizes the exact fixed-window certificate q*k^(n+L) < gap_L*(B-k), its iff with visible-prefix agreement at fixed (n, L), the necessary tail-mass lower bound q*k^(n+L) < B^L*(B-k), monotone persistence of the certificate under extra lookahead, the resulting finite carry/remainder output agreement layer, exact same-core transport of the first visible mismatch boundary in the k-power regime, and exact same-core transport of both the raw tail-mass inequality and the coarse k^(n+L) < modulus sufficient condition; the minimal/global theorem remains open

title: Canonical factorization of long division into orbit and carry
statement: The long-division DFA factors canonically into a remainder-orbit system together with a carry transducer for all coprime bases and moduli.
repo_status: tracked with explicit comparison interfaces, state-map candidate reports, bounded counterexample search, and an open-boundary statement separating state relabeling, quotient candidates, and the global theorem
